info.title: Decision Engine
info.description:
Open-source payment gateway routing service by Juspay. Selects the optimal payment processor for each transaction in real-time using success-rate scoring, rule-based routing, and elimination logic.

## Authentication

This API supports two authentication methods:

1. **JWT Bearer Token** - For dashboard/user sessions
   - Header: `Authorization: Bearer <jwt_token>`
   - Obtain via `/auth/login` endpoint

2. **API Key** - For service-to-service/programmatic access
   - Header: `x-api-key: DE_<64_char_hex>`
   - Create via `/api-key/create` endpoint

Protected endpoints require one of these authentication methods.

tags.description: Feed transaction outcomes back to improve SR scoring

get.description: Return rule-builder routing keys and supported values.

post.description:
Core routing decision API. Given a payment context and a list of eligible gateways, returns the optimal gateway to route to.

The engine applies a sequence of filters (currency, card brand, auth type, EMI, etc.) then scores remaining gateways using success rate history, elimination status, and contract obligations.

**Authentication:** Requires either JWT Bearer token or API Key.

multi_objective_routing.summary: Multi-objective routing — auth won (SR head retained)

multi_objective_info.reason: SR head retained — it is the highest expected-value PSP (3 ranked on EV).

multi_objective_cost_won.summary: Multi-objective routing — cost won (cheaper gateway promoted)

multi_objective_info.reason: Promoted 'adyen' over 'stripe' on expected value — saves 80.00 bps for 3.00pp auth.

get.description: Returns Up when the process is ready to serve traffic.

post.description: Register a new merchant account. The merchant ID is the primary identifier used in all subsequent routing and scoring calls.
Create and attach a merchant to the current operator user.
Activate a routing rule by ID for a merchant. Only one rule can be active at a time — activating a new rule deactivates the previous one.

200.description: Rule activated. The response body is empty.

post.description: Deactivate an active routing rule mapping by routing algorithm ID for a merchant. The call is idempotent: if the algorithm exists but is already inactive for the merchant, the API returns success.

200.description: Rule deactivated. The response body is empty.

400.description: Routing algorithm ID was not found.

post.description: Create a new Euclid declarative routing rule for a merchant. Supports `advanced` (full Euclid DSL), `priority` (ordered gateway list), `single` (fixed gateway), and `volume_split` (percentage-based distribution) algorithm types.
Evaluate the active routing rule for a merchant against a payment context. Returns the ordered list of gateways selected by the rule without consuming SR data.
Evaluate hybrid routing using the active routing rule plus runtime payment context.
List all routing rules for a merchant.
Retrieve the currently active routing rule for a merchant.
Create a service-level configuration for `successRate` or `elimination` scoring. Controls the time window, minimum data thresholds, and other parameters used in SR computation.
Delete a rule configuration.
Retrieve an existing rule configuration by merchant and type.
Update an existing rule configuration.

Feed a transaction outcome back into the success-rate model. Call this after every transaction so the engine has accurate SR data for future routing decisions.

A `CHARGED` status increases the gateway's SR; failure statuses (`AUTHENTICATION_FAILED`, `AUTHORIZATION_FAILED`, etc.) decrease it.
Legacy compatibility route. New integrations should use /update-gateway-score.

AdminSecret.name: x-admin-secret
AdminSecret.description: Admin bootstrap secret for creating merchant accounts

preferredGateway.description: Pins routing to a specific gateway when set.

paymentMethodType.description: Connector-defined payment method type, e.g. CARD, UPI, WALLET, NETBANKING.

paymentMethod.description: Connector-defined payment method, e.g. CREDIT, DEBIT, GPAY.

cardType.description: e.g. CREDIT, DEBIT, PREPAID, UPI, NB.

enableMultiObjective.description: Per-request override for the multi-objective (cost-aware) post-step. true forces it on, false forces it off; omitted falls back to the merchant's multi_objective_routing_enabled feature flag.

routing_approach.description: Routing approach used. SR_SELECTION_MULTI_OBJECTIVE indicates the multi-objective post-step promoted a cheaper gateway over the SR head.

multi_objective_info.description: Populated when the multi-objective post-step ran (multi_objective_routing_enabled feature flag or enableMultiObjective request field, outside hedging). Null otherwise.

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the rule.

data.description: Depends on type: array of ConnectorInfo ({gateway_name, gateway_id}) for `priority`; single ConnectorInfo for `single`; array of {split, output} for `volume_split`; Euclid AST Program for `advanced`; ABTestData ({control_algorithm_id, variant_algorithm_id, variant_split_pct, min_sample_size, guardrail_threshold_pp, variant_sr_config?, control_sr_config?}) for `ab_test`

metadata.description: Arbitrary metadata attached to the rule.

config.description: Tagged config variant. `type` is `successRate`, `elimination`, or `debitRouting`. `data` holds the variant-specific fields.

MultiObjectiveInfo.description: Present when the multi-objective (cost-aware) post-step ran. Explains why the gateway was picked: the SR head was kept (AUTH_WON) or a higher economic-value PSP was promoted (COST_WON). Economic value is auth rate × settlement value, where settlement value = txn amount − cost of payment processing (acquirer, issuer & network fee).

reason.example: Promoted 'adyen' over 'stripe' on expected value — saves 80.00 bps for 3.00pp auth.

srHead.description: The PSP the SR scorer would have picked.

chosen.description: The PSP the post-step actually chose. Equals srHead when auth won.

costSavedBps.description: Fee saved in bps when outcome is COST_WON (srHead.costBps - chosen.costBps).

qualifiedCount.description: Number of PSPs that had cost data and were ranked on expected value.

margin.description: Merchant margin (fraction of ticket) applied for this transaction. Configured via the successRate config margin field; defaults to 1.0.

evGapTop2.description: Expected-value gap between the top-two EV-ranked PSPs, as a fraction of ticket. Null when fewer than two PSPs had cost data.

ClusterDims.description: Cluster dimension breakdown (connector, card network, funding, issuer country, currency, IC category).

fields.description: Success-rate routing dimensions. Must be from the eligible set.

ValueType.description: Euclid condition value. Tagged by `type`; `value` holds the payload.

value.description: Shape depends on `type`: number->integer, enum_variant/str_value/global_ref->string, number_array/enum_variant_array->array of number/string, number_comparison_array->array of comparisons.

RoutingRequest.description: Request body for POST /routing/evaluate (src/euclid/types.rs::RoutingRequest). No camelCase rename — snake_case field names.

fallback_output.description: Used when the rule evaluation yields no eligible connector.

parameters.description: Map of DSL parameter name (e.g. currency, amount, cardType) to its ValueType.
